fields.value: 30,000 PwC professionals trained and certified on Claude
Enterprises currently lose more than $2 trillion annually operating on pre-AI systems and processes

value: Model Context Protocol integration for secure enterprise data connectivity
Claude Code for software engineering and automation tasks
Claude Cowork for collaborative professional workflows
Office of the CFO business unit
Three active AI incubation pods: Finance, Supply Chain, and Deal Making

evidence.quote: This unit targets finance transformation in regulated industries — banking, insurance, and healthcare — where accuracy, auditability, and compliance are non-negotiable.
Insurance underwriting : Claim review cycles reduced from 10 weeks to 10 days, representing a roughly 86% reduction in cycle time.

The joint Center of Excellence will serve as the operational hub for this deployment, coordinating training, certification, quality standards, and ongoing development of Claude-powered solutions across PwC's service lines.

The expanded partnership includes training and certifying 30,000 PwC professionals on Claude, establishing a joint Center of Excellence, launching a new "Office of the CFO" business unit built on Claude, and extending rollout toward PwC's global workforce of 364,000 employees.

PwC estimates that enterprises currently lose more than $2 trillion annually operating on pre-AI systems and processes — a figure the firm positions as the addressable market for the AI-native services it intends to build through this partnership.

By enabling Claude to connect directly to enterprise data sources — client records, financial systems, case management platforms — rather than requiring manual data uploads, MCP reduces friction in deploying Claude across regulated industries where data governance is a primary concern.
Model Context Protocol integration enables secure enterprise data connectivity without requiring custom API development

PwC's rollout centers on two core Claude products: Claude Code for software engineering and automation tasks, and Claude Cowork for collaborative professional workflows. These tools are being integrated into ChatPwC, the firm's internal AI assistant platform, and connected to enterprise data systems through Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP).
